title: Jerusalem Post Privacy Policy
content:
Jerusalem Post Online Terms of Service, Revision 1.1, Last modified january 27th, 2021.
Preamble.
These Terms of Service govern your use of the Jerusalem Post website, purchases you make through the website and any content contribution you may make to the website. While the formal and full agreement governs your use of the website, The Jerusalem Post has posted, for your convenience and use, a brief summary of your rights, duties and obligations:
-
You may use the site to read content, post user comments, purchase premium subscriptions or content and register to any of our newsletters.
-
All of our subscription services are periodical recurring, and will be billed on a billing cycle.
-
Premium accounts are meant for personal use, and no resale, lending or reuse of accounts is allowed.
-
Any submission you may post shall be non-offensive.
-
The Jerusalem Post may charge fees for parts of the services.
-
Some personal data about you may be saved according to the Jerusalem Post’s Privacy Policy.
-
No matter what, The Jerusalem Post shall never be liable for any damage incurred by using this website or service.
We recommend that you read the full terms of service prior to posting any content to the website, registering to any newsletter or purchasing premium content.
Scope of Services.
These Terms of Service are a binding agreement between The Jerusalem Post Ltd., a duly incorporated Israeli corporation, whose principal place of business is 13 Haachim Misalvita, Israel (“The Jerusalem Post”) and yourself, an individual or a person acting on behalf of a corporation or organization which authorized you in order to enter into these terms of service (“You”).
For the purpose of these Terms of Service, the term “Service” shall refer to any of the following services provided by The Jerusalem Post, as described in this clause, including any of their features.
Content, News & Updates:
The Jerusalem Post may provide you with access to articles, news updates, opinion columns and other content, all through its website located at https://www.jpost.com (“Website”). Such content shall be provided either by The Jerusalem Post or by chosen affiliates and third parties, where marked as such. Any commercial or advertorial content shall be marked as “Special Content”.
User Submissions and User Generated Content.
The Jerusalem Post may provide you with an option to contribute and post your commentary in regards to News, Updates, Blogs or other users contributions or commentaries.
Premium Content
The Jerusalem Post may provide you with additional content, commentary and information, all defined as “Premium Content” including online editions of the daily "Jerusalem Post" electronic paper, and a selection of exclusive articles from "The Jerusalem Report," The Jerusalem Post's youth magazine "Dash," The Jerusalem Post's "Christian Edition," an easy-Hebrew magazine "Ivrit,", The Jerusalem Post's weekend magazines and more. Such access shall be made against payments and fees, as described in these Terms of Service
If you purchase iOS Premium Content via our iOS app, which includes premium subscription to premium content, it shall be done using the App Store payment method. Payments shall be charged monthly, on a recurring basis, and shall be US$5 per month.
Newsletter
The Jerusalem Post may provide you with an option to receive timely updates through Electronic mail, including promotional offers and other relevant information. When doing so, you shall be required to enter some relevant information, so that The Jerusalem Post may provide you with better services and promotional activity.
Following the Registration process, an email may be sent to your email box to confirm and authenticate that you requested to register to the Newsletter.
From time to time, and according to your preferences, The Jerusalem Post shall send you updates through electronic mail which may include both the Jerusalem Post’s content, third party content and advertisments
Electronic Edition.
The Jerusalem Post may provide you with an interface to access a digital edition of the Jerusalem Post Print Edition; such access shall be made payments and fees, all as described.
Archive
The Jerusalem Post may provide you with access to on-line archives of news articles and any other information stored on the Website.
Polls
The Jerusalem Post may present, from time to time, user polls which shall present its users' views and opinions in regards to statements or questions of interest. You may participate once in each poll. you acknowledge and consent that The Jerusalem Post may track and monitor your votes and present them to its users, as well as track attempts to defraud it.
The The Jerusalem Post may avail the results of such polls and may allow access to its polling archive. you acknowledge that the polls made by The Jerusalem Post do not reflect the general public, nor do they reflect the users of The Jerusalem Post's website, as voting in polls is voluntary and not mandatory.
News Ticker
The Jerusalem Post may avail to you, all with accordance to the features inherent in the code, a short HTML code which you may install, at your own discretion and risk, at any website you may control or own.
You are hereby granted a limited, non-exclusive, worldwide, temporary, revocable, non-sublicensable, nontransferable and non-assignable license to use and display such HTML code at websites, according to the stipulations and restrictions setforth.
However, you hereby undertake to incur and bear all risk associated with the execution of such computer code.
You may not amend, modify or otherwise create any derivative works from the HTML code, including any code meant to exploit any vulnerability in Website, inject malicious content or computer code, circumvent The Jerusalem Post's server, or otherwise create any work falsifying the origin of the content in the News Ticker.
Any use of the News-Ticker shall be where The Jerusalem Post is properly credited for the origin of the news.
Sharing and Social Features
The Jerusalem Post may you with an Interface to share articles to your friends by electronic correspondence or other social features.
Classified Ads
The Jerusalem Post may avail you to an interface to publish and/or search classified ads, either for real estate, employment or other matters. In such case, the Jerusalem Post may charge rates in accordance to article 3 to these Terms and the content of any classified ad you submitted shall be subject to the Jerusalem Post's Contribution Policy as set forth in article 4 to these terms.
Additional Services
The Jerusalem Post may, from time to time, add additional services and portions to the Website, which may include additional terms.
Fees
Services are provided either free of charge or against a set fee, all with consideration of the Service's scope and field. you hereby acknowledge that the free nature of a specific Service is not guaranteed and should any Service incur any costs, you shall pay them in order to use that Service.
The Jerusalem Post may, from time to time, set fees as deemed in each Service. The fees set-forth are for non-commercial and personal uses of Service and not for commercial effort or endeavor. Such fees may be monthly, daily or yearly, and you shall be billed according to the fee policy of such service.
All payments according to these Terms of Service shall be made by a payment method which you own and for purchase of subscription, personalized service or content. By agreeing to pay or committing to purchase, you may be required to provide The Jerusalem Post with your Credit Card information, which shall be stored securely.
Subscriptions
When you register to a subscription service, such as The Jerusalem Post's Premium Service or Electronic Edition Subscriptions, you hereby grant The Jerusalem Post permission to bill you on each calendar month for the services rendered on that specific month. The payment shall be processed by The Jerusalem Post and until you shall notify The Jerusalem Post that you wish to cancel such subscription.
Refunds
As content is considered intangible goods under Israeli Law, The Jerusalem Post shall by no mean, cause or reason issue any refund, rebate, discount, credit or other form of payment to you, should you decide to annul, cancel or void your subscription. All your refund rights under applicable laws shall supersede these Terms.
User Accounts and Use
In order to use some of the Services, you will be required to register and open User Account(s), where different accounts may be used for different services. When signing up for a User Account and when using each of the services, you hereby undertake to comply with the following stipulations, as well as any other reasonable orders by The Jerusalem Post, as you may be instructed from time to time.
Eligibility
You are eligible to enter into these Terms of Service and: (i) were not prohibited by any authorized authority, judicial order to law into entering any agreement; (ii) are over 18 years of age or obtained consent to enter into these Terms of Service from your parent or guardian; (iii) were not previously prohibited by The Jerusalem Post from using Services or held another User Account; (iv) do not reside in and proscribed proscribed state or member of any proscribed organization; and (v) have all proper authorization, if you are acting on behalf of a corporation, into entering into these Terms of Service.
Personal Use
Your use of the Services shall be personal and non-commercial; you shall not transfer, distribute or publish any content or portion of the Services to any third parties apart from where available through Service itself (such as in the news-ticker or RSS feed) and shall not sell, resell, syndicate or republish any of the Services.
Authentication
When registering to the Services, you may be required to authenticate your method of payment, email address or both in order to make sure that emails will be received and payments shall be made.
You hereby warrant that any information you provided to The Jerusalem Post is true and correct. you shall not use the Services to impersonate anyone else or to register on behalf of any other person.
Use
You will use the Services only in a bona-fide manner, and avoid any use which may obstruct, interfere or delay the Services to any other person. you will not use the Services to spam, send unsolicited publications, stalk, harass or threaten any other person or computer system.
You will not use any automated means, including computer code, crawlers, robots or scripts in order to harvest information, parse, cache or otherwise provide service to any third person. you will not create or use any script, software, code or program that otherwise manipulates, falsifies, adds or removes functionality to the Services.
Intellectual Property & Licenses
Apart from where specifically permitted, nothing in these Terms of Service shall constitute as a license to use any portion of copyrighted material titled by The Jerusalem Post; you will not modify, create derivative works, distribute or otherwise amend the Services, apart from where specifically permitted, any portion of the Service.
News Ticker
When using the News-Ticker in any of your websites, you hereby grant The Jerusalem Post the right to track, monitor and inspect any elements and any visits to such website, as well as retain statistical and analytical information, all as made available by The Jerusalem Post's services.
RSS Feed
You may use The Jerusalem Post's RSS feed in order to create any application, service or website as well as other services, as long as such services do not crawl or otherwise parse, display or publish any content from the Website which was not presented in the RSS feed. you may use and display the images attached to the RSS feed in your website, application or service only with the content and text attached to it.
Newsletter
